channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is set to begin on Sunday, pausing the devastating 15-month war between Israel and the militant Hamas group in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ski lift accident leaves 30 injured at Spanish resort in the Pyrenees
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — At least 30 people have been injured in a ski lift accident at the Spanish resort of Astún, in the Pyrenees mountain range, emergency services for Spain's northern Aragon region said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stefanik wages Democratic charm offensive ahead of confirmation hearing
item.description: Senate Democrats appear to be warming up to Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.), President-elect Trump’s combative and confrontational nominee for envoy to the United Nations, encouraged by commitments she’ll engage with the global body rather than seek to burn it down.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Gunman shoots dead 2 judges in Iran's capital tied to 1988 mass executions
item.description: Iranian officials say a man fatally shot two prominent hard-line judges in Tehran, both of whom allegedly took part in the mass execution of dissidents in 1988.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel, Hamas to go into effect Sunday morning, Qatar says
item.description: The cease-fire between Hamas and Israel is expected to go into effect on Sunday at 8:30 a.m. local time, Qatar’s foreign ministry announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump vowed to end the war in Ukraine quickly but Moscow and Kyiv are digging in before any talks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has pledged to broker a peace deal in Ukraine, but as he prepares to take office, peace seems as elusive as ever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: More than 40 Pakistanis feared drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off African coast
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — More than 40 Pakistanis were feared to have drowned in the capsizing of a boat off West Africa's Atlantic coastline, which has emerged as a primary point of departure for migrants aiming to reach Europe.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Incoming British ambassador to US praises Trump in op-ed
item.description: The incoming British ambassador to the U.S. Peter Mandelson praised President-elect Trump in a new op-ed, predicting his forthcoming presidency will be one of the “most consequential” in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: UN announces new plan to counter the surge in antisemitism
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations announced new actions Friday to counter the surge in antisemitism, including encouraging governments to enforce laws against hate crimes and discrimination. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel's Cabinet approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet approved a deal early Saturday for a ceasefire in Gaza that would release dozens of hostages held there and pause the 15-month war with Hamas, bringing the sides a step closer to ending their deadliest and most destructive fighting ever.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli government approves ceasefire deal: Reports
item.description: The Israeli government has formally approved the ceasefire deal in Gaza and the release of some hostages, soon halting the conflict that has been raging for 15 months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Blessings for dogs? Bring them to Mexico City's cathedral and St. Anthony will do the rest
item.description: Behaving at their best, a dozen dogs attended Mass at Mexico City’s cathedral, waiting for their turn to be doused with holy water.
item.dc:creator: María Teresa Hernández, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli security experts argue ‘bad' deal with Hamas, but ‘no other way’ to free hostages
item.description: Security experts analyzing the deal brokered between Israel and Hamas argue it was a 'bad deal' as it could pose security risks for Israel but one that needed to be done to return the hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British PM Starmer visits Auschwitz, vows to fight antisemitism ahead of security talks in Poland
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday visited the Nazi German extermination camp at Auschwitz, voicing his “sheer horror” at what he saw there, before holding talks with Poland's leaders on stepping up European defense and tightening Britain's ties with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Iran sign a partnership treaty to deepen their ties in the face of Western sanctions
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin and his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian, signed a broad cooperation pact Friday as their countries deepened their partnership in the face of stinging Western sanctions.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, Iran to seal partnership treaty days before Trump takes office
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian will meet in Moscow to sign a partnership pact as the two nations brace for President-elect Trump's return.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel, Hamas ceasefire poised to begin Sunday
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is poised to begin Sunday, after the Israeli security Cabinet on Friday endorsed the deal covering it and the release of hostages and called for the full government to agree to the terms.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: France's Macron expresses support for new leadership in Lebanon as it recovers from crisis
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has met with Lebanon’s newly elected President Joseph Aoun and vowed to support the small nation as it attempts to recover from the 14-month Israel-Hezbollah war and an economic crisis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canada sends Trump 'message' with retaliatory tariff talk
item.description: Canada must send a “message” to President-elect Trump on tariffs, one of the country's provincial leaders said Wednesday alongside Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, who described the threatened levies as unnecessary.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Israel aims to vote Saturday night on accepting ceasefire, hostage deal
item.description: Israel’s government is aiming to vote Saturday night local time to accept the terms of a ceasefire and hostage agreement, multiple outlets reported, delaying implementation of a deal that negotiators hoped to go into effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu delays Gaza cease-fire vote, accusing Hamas of trying to back out of deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday accused Hamas of backing out of a cease-fire deal to release hostages and bring a pause to more than a year of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says ceasefire on hold until hostages' names released
item.description: The deal, including the release of 33 hostages, has been approved by Israel but Benjamin Netanyahu said it won't go ahead until names are released.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: 84-year-old man fights off robber using pair of jeans
item.description: YORKSHIRE, U.K. (WFLA) — An 84-year-old man used a pair of jeans to fight off a would-be robber as he did his laundry in South Yorkshire.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation
